summary: Iteration 8 frontend testing complete: validated (1) Trainer Customizer modal with full color picker UX and (2) new cartoony ARFallbackScene shown when camera is off. Both features work as specified. Strong visual confirmation via screenshot showing yellow-haired customized trainer overlaid on the new AR fallback scene (sun, clouds, hills, grass, pokestop pillar, gym tower).
verified_features: MapPage: data-testid='customize-trainer-btn' renders as amber Shirt FAB. Bounding rect cx=262 sits LEFT of locate-me-btn lx=326 (mobile 390x844). Both clickable.
Modal renders all 6 color rows with expected swatch counts: cap=8, shirt=8, shorts=8, skin=6, hair=8, ring=6 (total 44 swatches matching PALETTES in TrainerCustomizer.jsx).
Footer buttons all present: customizer-randomize, customizer-reset, customizer-save, customizer-close.

Live preview SVG inside modal updates immediately when a swatch is clicked: confirmed stop-color values for #EF4444 (shirt) and #DC2626 (cap) appear in the radialGradient stops of the in-modal trainer-avatar after click.
Save persists JSON to localStorage under exact key 'rrdc_avatar_colors_<camperId>'. Verified key for camper b9d35aaf-90f9-5c06-8674-3f60999d1846 contained the saved color object after save click.
When localStorage is preseeded with custom colors, /map renders my-location-avatar with those colors applied (yellow hair fill #F4D03F visible in screenshot).

ARPage: data-testid='ar-fallback-scene' renders when camera is off/denied. Initial fallback present:true (camera denied in headless). After clicking toggle-camera-btn the scene remains correctly rendered.

ARFallbackScene structure verified via DOM: 9 svg elements, 17 ellipses (clouds), 59 paths (hills/grass), 1 linearGradient (grass gradient). All cartoony elements (sun, drifting clouds, rolling hills, grass tufts, pokestop pillar, gym tower, vignette) confirmed visually in screenshot — NOT the old plain dark gradient.
Backend regression: GET /api/spawn/current returns >=4 spawns near camp center as established in iteration 7.
action_items: Optional: TrainerCustomizer.jsx useEffect re-runs setColors(loadAvatarColors(camperId)) whenever camperId changes. If user.id loads asynchronously AFTER the modal is opened (rare race), the user's in-progress selections could be reset. Consider gating the reload to only fire on the open->true transition, e.g. check a ref so it doesn't re-run when camperId arrives later. Low priority — did not reproduce reliably.
critical_code_review_comments: ARFallbackScene.jsx line 67: animate uses window.innerWidth at render time inside framer-motion animate prop. On window resize this value is stale (closure captures initial size). For a fallback scene this is acceptable but worth noting.

TrainerCustomizer.jsx: useEffect resets colors when modal re-opens which is correct UX, but it also runs whenever camperId changes which could clobber unsaved edits if auth context reconciles late. Minor.
TrainerAvatar.jsx: walking prop is wired through MapPage from a live geolocation movement detector — that's a nice touch (was a future-work item from iteration 7, now addressed).
MapPage.jsx loadAvatarColors falls back to DEFAULT_COLORS spread — partial saves (e.g. older clients with fewer keys) gracefully merge. Good defensive coding.
test_credentials: Camper login via GET /api/groups/B01/campers → POST /api/camper/login {camper_id}. Used Colton Ahern (id b9d35aaf-90f9-5c06-8674-3f60999d1846).
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded campers and pokemon roster. One spawn was triggered via GET /api/spawn/current?lat=&lng= for the AR test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For trainer customization: GET /api/groups/B01/campers, POST /api/camper/login, store token in localStorage as 'rrdc_user_token', set 'rrdc_onboarded_<camperId>'='1'. To test customization without UI clicks, write JSON colors directly to localStorage key 'rrdc_avatar_colors_<camperId>' and reload /map. data-testids: customize-trainer-btn, trainer-customizer-modal, customizer-save/reset/randomize/close, color-{part}-{HEX_NO_HASH} for each swatch. For AR fallback: navigate to /ar?spawn=<id>, [data-testid='ar-fallback-scene'] is present whenever camera is not running (denied/off). Headless Chrome cannot grant camera, so fallback is always visible there. Geolocation in iframe needs add_init_script with Object.defineProperty(navigator,'geolocation',{configurable:true, value:...}).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs to fix. Both new features (TrainerCustomizer + ARFallbackScene) work end-to-end.
